---
title: "Proposed Solutions"
subtitle: "Closing the 51.3% unrecognized gap — a prioritised roadmap"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
import polars as pl
import plotly.graph_objects as go

CSV = '/Users/omkarmalpure/Documents/broadjump_v2/analyze-data/UOM-38552.csv'
df  = pl.read_csv(CSV, null_values=['', 'NULL'])

unrecog_df = df.filter(pl.col('IsRecognized') == False)
total      = len(df)
unrecog    = len(unrecog_df)

no_ph   = len(unrecog_df.filter(pl.col('TotalPurchaseQuantity') == 0))
low_ph  = len(unrecog_df.filter((pl.col('TotalPurchaseQuantity') > 0) & (pl.col('TotalPurchaseQuantity') < 5)))
some_ph = len(unrecog_df.filter(pl.col('TotalPurchaseQuantity') >= 5))

FONT  = 'Inter, sans-serif'
SFONT = 'Space Grotesk, sans-serif'
```

## The Unrecognized Population

```{python}
#| echo: false
fig_u = go.Figure(go.Pie(
    labels=['No/Zero Purchase History', 'Very Low History (1–4 units)', 'Price Out of DBSCAN Range'],
    values=[no_ph, low_ph, some_ph],
    marker=dict(
        colors=['#ef4444', '#f97316', '#f59e0b'],
        line=dict(color='white', width=3),
    ),
    textinfo='label+percent',
    textfont=dict(family=FONT, size=11),
    insidetextorientation='radial',
    hole=0.5,
    hovertemplate='<b>%{label}</b><br>%{value:,} rows<br>%{percent}<extra></extra>',
))
fig_u.add_annotation(
    text=f'<b style="font-size:20px">{unrecog:,}</b><br><span style="color:#64748b;font-size:11px">Unrecognized</span>',
    x=0.5, y=0.5, showarrow=False,
    font=dict(family=SFONT, size=13, color='#0f172a'), align='center',
)
fig_u.update_layout(
    paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='white',
    font=dict(family=FONT, color='#334155'),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=-0.18, xanchor='center', x=0.5,
                font=dict(size=11), bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)'),
    margin=dict(t=20, b=80, l=20, r=20),
    height=380,
    showlegend=True,
)
fig_u.show()
```

| Root Cause | Rows Affected | Proposed Strategy |
|:-----------|:-------------:|:------------------|
| No / zero purchase history | `{python} f"{no_ph:,}"` | Solution A — Agentic web search |
| Very low history (1–4 units) | `{python} f"{low_ph:,}"` | Solution B — Similar-product transfer |
| Price outside DBSCAN range | `{python} f"{some_ph:,}"` | Solution C — Threshold relaxation |

---

## Solution A — Agentic Web Search

<div class="solution-cards">
<div class="sol-card sol-a">
<div class="sol-header">
  <div class="sol-icon">🤖</div>
  <div class="sol-top">
    <div class="sol-label">Solution A · Zero-History Products</div>
    <div class="sol-title">Agentic Web Search with Citation</div>
    <div class="sol-reach">Targets <strong>`{python} f"{no_ph:,}"`</strong> rows with no purchase history</div>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="sol-body">
<p>When a product has no purchase history at all, Step 5 has no anchors. The only signal available is the product description, manufacturer, and catalog number. An agentic pipeline can autonomously enrich these rows:</p>
<ul class="sol-steps">
  <li data-n="1"><strong>Query construction</strong> — build a structured search from <code>RecognizedProductDesc</code>, <code>RecognizedManufacturerName</code>, and <code>RecognizedProductCatalogNumber</code>.</li>
  <li data-n="2"><strong>Search & extract</strong> — agent calls a web search tool or medical-device catalogue API and extracts candidate UOM labels and typical pack sizes.</li>
  <li data-n="3"><strong>Citation attachment</strong> — every suggestion is stored with its source URL so analysts can verify before accepting.</li>
  <li data-n="4"><strong>Confidence flagging</strong> — agent rates its own confidence ("exact catalog number match" vs. "description keyword match"). Low-confidence results require mandatory analyst sign-off before write-back.</li>
  <li data-n="5"><strong>Feedback loop</strong> — accepted suggestions are written to purchase history so future runs resolve the same products without a search.</li>
</ul>
<div class="sol-tags">
  <span class="sol-tag effort-high">High effort</span>
  <span class="sol-tag">Requires analyst review</span>
  <span class="sol-tag">Self-improving over time</span>
  <span class="sol-tag">Needs prompt fine-tuning</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

---

## Solution B — Similar-Product Transfer

<div class="solution-cards">
<div class="sol-card sol-b">
<div class="sol-header">
  <div class="sol-icon">🔗</div>
  <div class="sol-top">
    <div class="sol-label">Solution B · Sparse History Products</div>
    <div class="sol-title">Purchase History Bridge via Product Similarity</div>
    <div class="sol-reach">Targets <strong>`{python} f"{low_ph:,}"`</strong> rows with 1–4 unit history</div>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="sol-body">
<p>Rather than going to the web, look inside existing purchase history for structurally similar products and borrow their ThresholdV2 clusters as a prior:</p>
<ul class="sol-steps">
  <li data-n="1"><strong>Similarity candidates</strong> — find products in the catalogue sharing the same <code>RecognizedManufacturerID</code>, same <code>RecognizedProductTypeID</code>, and similar description tokens (keyword overlap or embedding distance).</li>
  <li data-n="2"><strong>UOM profile transfer</strong> — the similar product's ThresholdV2 clusters and WAP are borrowed. The proximity scoring in Step 5 runs exactly as normal, using the borrowed thresholds.</li>
  <li data-n="3"><strong>Downgraded confidence</strong> — the resulting score is multiplied by a transfer discount factor (e.g., 0.80) to signal the assignment is inferred, not directly observed.</li>
  <li data-n="4"><strong>Analyst review queue</strong> — transferred rows surface in a separate queue for analysts to confirm the analogy is valid before the result propagates.</li>
</ul>
<div class="sol-tags">
  <span class="sol-tag effort-med">Medium effort</span>
  <span class="sol-tag">Needs similarity criteria co-design with analysts</span>
  <span class="sol-tag">No external API calls</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

### Key questions for analysts

Before implementing, these must be answered collaboratively:

- Does same **manufacturer + product type** constitute a strong enough analogy, or are there product categories where this would be systematically misleading?
- What **discount factor** appropriately penalises borrowed confidence?
- Are there clinical categories (implants vs. consumables) that require different similarity criteria?

---

## Solution C — Threshold Relaxation

<div class="solution-cards">
<div class="sol-card sol-c">
<div class="sol-header">
  <div class="sol-icon">⚙️</div>
  <div class="sol-top">
    <div class="sol-label">Solution C · Price Out-of-Range Products</div>
    <div class="sol-title">Widen the Proximity Window + Analyst Escalation</div>
    <div class="sol-reach">Targets <strong>`{python} f"{some_ph:,}"`</strong> rows with history but score &lt; 0.30</div>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="sol-body">
<p>These rows have adequate purchase history but the file's <code>PriceUOM</code> lands too far from every DBSCAN cluster. Common causes: supplier price changes, new pack sizes, or unit vs. case mismatches. Two complementary approaches:</p>
<ul class="sol-steps">
  <li data-n="C1"><strong>Widen LOG_EPS</strong> — raising <code>LOG_EPS</code> from 0.25 → 0.40 extends the tolerance from ~1.78× to ~2.51× price deviation. This is a global change; must be validated on a hold-out set to measure false-positive impact before deployment.</li>
  <li data-n="C2"><strong>Price-trend adjustment</strong> — for products with time-stamped history, detect upward/downward price trends and project cluster ranges forward. A product trending $10→$13→$16 is likely ~$19 today; the 12-month-old cluster misses it.</li>
  <li data-n="C3"><strong>Analyst escalation queue</strong> — rows where the best candidate scores 0.20–0.30 (just below acceptance) are surfaced to analysts with the best DBSCAN candidate and distance from the cluster edge visible. Fast accept/reject with feedback loops back to threshold calibration.</li>
</ul>
<div class="sol-tags">
  <span class="sol-tag effort-low">C1: Low effort (config change)</span>
  <span class="sol-tag effort-low">C3: Low effort (UI change)</span>
  <span class="sol-tag effort-med">C2: Medium effort</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

---

## Coverage Comparison

```{python}
#| echo: false
sol_labels = [
    'C3 — Escalation Queue<br><i style="color:#94a3b8;font-size:10px">Near-miss rows, score 0.20–0.30</i>',
    'C1 — Widen LOG_EPS<br><i style="color:#94a3b8;font-size:10px">Config change + hold-out validation</i>',
    'B — Similar-Product Transfer<br><i style="color:#94a3b8;font-size:10px">Sparse history bridge</i>',
    'A — Agentic Web Search<br><i style="color:#94a3b8;font-size:10px">Zero-history enrichment</i>',
]
sol_values = [int(some_ph*0.6), int(some_ph*0.4), low_ph, no_ph]
sol_colors = ['#10b981', '#06b6d4', '#4f46e5', '#8b5cf6']
effort_map = ['Low', 'Low', 'Medium', 'High']

fig_s = go.Figure()
fig_s.add_trace(go.Bar(
    y=sol_labels, x=sol_values, orientation='h',
    marker=dict(color=sol_colors, line=dict(width=0)),
    text=[f'  ~{v:,} rows' for v in sol_values],
    textposition='outside',
    textfont=dict(family=FONT, size=11, color='#475569'),
    hovertemplate='<b>%{y}</b><br>~%{x:,} rows potentially recovered<extra></extra>',
    cliponaxis=False,
))
fig_s.update_layout(
    paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='#fafafa',
    font=dict(family=FONT, color='#334155', size=11),
    xaxis=dict(
        gridcolor='#f1f5f9', linecolor='#e2e8f0',
        tickfont=dict(color='#64748b', size=11),
        range=[0, max(sol_values) * 1.22],
        title='Estimated Rows Recoverable',
    ),
    yaxis=dict(
        linecolor='#e2e8f0',
        tickfont=dict(family=FONT, size=11, color='#1e293b'),
        autorange='reversed', title='',
    ),
    margin=dict(t=20, b=50, l=260, r=110),
    bargap=0.35, height=340,
    hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor='#0f172a', font_color='white', font_family=FONT),
)
fig_s.show()
```

---

## Recommended Roadmap

<div class="roadmap">
<div class="roadmap-item">
  <div class="roadmap-horizon">Medium Term</div>
  <div class="roadmap-title">B — Similar-Product Transfer</div>
  <div class="roadmap-desc">Co-design similarity criteria with domain analysts. Pilot on one manufacturer/category before expanding. Borrow ThresholdV2 profiles from structurally similar products with rich history.</div>
</div>
<div class="roadmap-item">
  <div class="roadmap-horizon">Longer Term</div>
  <div class="roadmap-title">A — Agentic Web Search</div>
  <div class="roadmap-desc">Prototype on a small batch of zero-history products. Evaluate citation quality and build the analyst review workflow. Requires prompt engineering, de-duplication, and a write-back feedback loop before scaling.</div>
</div>
</div>

| Solution | Reach | Effort | Analyst Input Needed |
|:---------|------:|:------:|:--------------------:|
| B — Similar-product transfer | `{python} f"~{low_ph:,}"` rows | Medium | Yes — criteria definition |
| A — Agentic search | `{python} f"~{no_ph:,}"` rows | High | Yes — citation sign-off |
